rationale: Both correctly tie the failure to PR #4834's new wake_up() guard matching yenuo26's bisection, and both cite the same real guard code in async_omni.py, but both overclaim resolution — asserting the test fix is 'already on main' and recommending closure — when the actual thread shows Flink-ddd still asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode run, i.e. unresolved. Y is slightly better grounded (cites the generate() partial-wake guard at async_omni.py:319-324, specific test line numbers for the regression tests) and more complete (adds runtime guidance for users, cleanly organized) versus X's redundant/duplicated answer text and thinner sourcing beyond the core diff.
